frame_analyses.description: Purple 2-0 silk suture visible between gloved hands in overhead position. Yellow Penrose drain block visible at top with marked targets.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase, suture and workpiece clearly visible, appropriate staging

frame_analyses.description: Suture being passed through first marked target on Penrose drain. Right hand holding suture end, left hand stabilizing drain block.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through target mark 1, good drain stabilization

frame_analyses.description: Suture passed through both marked targets. Purple suture creating loop through drain. Yellow and peach suture tails visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both passes completed, suture tension appears appropriate

frame_analyses.description: Weston knot formation begins. Left hand holding one suture tail, right hand beginning wrap around for first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Labeled 'Weston knot' on screen, demonstrating extracorporeal knot technique

frame_analyses.description: First throw knot fully formed. Suture wrapped and cinched around itself. Purple and yellow tails clearly visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean knot formation, appropriate tension observed

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed. Both hands manipulating suture tails to create wrap around main suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand position demonstrates proper technique for sequential throws

frame_analyses.description: Second throw completed and cinched. Multiple wraps visible around suture.
Third throw formation. Hands repositioning to create final wrap. Drain block visible above with suture passing through both marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw in progress, hands switching appropriately

frame_analyses.description: All three throws complete. Knot appears bulky with multiple wraps visible. Yellow tail being held by right hand.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete knot sequence visible, knot appears secure and well-formed

frame_analyses.description: Knot positioned at drain. Suture showing traction on drain with knot sitting at closure point. Purple tail visible on left.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears to be at appropriate closure position on drain slit

frame_analyses.description: Scissors visible preparing to cut suture tails. Right hand holding scissors, left hand stabilizing knot and suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final cutting phase, appropriate instrument control

frame_analyses.description: End of video shows completed task with tied knot at drain and suture tails cut. Title card shows 'World Laparoscopy Hospital - DR RK MISHRA'

penalties.description: No observable suture deviation, knot slippage, drain avulsion, or slit gap detected

knot_assessments.notes: First throw well-formed with appropriate tension
Second throw adds security, hand switched appropriately from throw 1
Third throw completes knot sequence with good tension, hand switched from throw 2

confidence_rationale: Video classification as expert_demo confirmed by narration label 'DR RK MISHRA' and 'World Laparoscopy Hospital', clean overhead camera angles throughout, excellent visibility of suture passes and knot formation. Suture placement clearly on target marks. Knot progression clearly visible with no slippage or tension loss. Final closure complete and secure.
technique_summary: Expert-level extracorporeal knot tying demonstration. Suture passage through both marked targets is precise and on-target. Three throws are formed cleanly with appropriate hand switching. Knot security is excellent with no observed slippage. Final slit closure is complete. Task completed in 98 seconds, substantially faster than proficiency benchmark of 136 seconds, indicating expert performance.
strengths: Extremely efficient suture passage through both targets with minimal positioning adjustments
Clean, controlled extracorporeal knot formation with consistent tension throughout all three throws
Proper hand switching between throws demonstrates bilateral dexterity
Final knot positioned precisely at intended closure site with complete slit approximation
Completion time of 98 seconds is 28% faster than proficiency benchmark
Excellent drain stabilization throughout procedure preventing any avulsion risk

demonstrated_techniques: Two-handed stabilization of Penrose drain during suture passage
Perpendicular suture entry through marked targets
Extracorporeal knot formation using suture tail wrapping technique
Progressive throw sequence (throw 1, throw 2, throw 3) with alternating hand dominance
Appropriate tension maintenance to prevent slippage while avoiding over-tension
Knot positioning directly at marked closure point on drain slit
Bilateral hand coordination for suture manipulation outside trainer
verbal_cues: Frame label 'Weston knot' identifies knot type being demonstrated
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding suture deviation from marked targets through careful passage technique
Preventing knot slippage by maintaining consistent tension during throw sequence
Preventing drain avulsion by gentle handling and proper stabilization
Ensuring complete slit closure by positioning knot at exact closure point
Proper hand positioning to create secure wraps without tangling

improvement_suggestions: This is an expert demonstration; no improvements needed for instructional purposes
